reasoning: The stock recently experienced strong downward momentum, dropping from around 55.10 to 50.86 over the days leading up to August 1. General news around Delta and airlines currently points to sluggish travel demand and macroeconomic pressures causing guidance cuts and downgrades. The yfinance tool gives a combined 1-week estimate of 0.44 derived from recent momentum, seasonal trends (airlines tend to weaken in late summer), and technical levels. Since stock moves over a short horizon remain largely unpredictable but heavily influenced by very short-term momentum and negative sentiment, I assigned an initial near-term probability of 0.45. In accordance with standard forecasting principles for unpredictable financial markets, uncertainty naturally increases with the forecast horizon. The upward equity risk premium balances the negative short-term momentum, so projections organically regress towards a coin toss (0.50). Therefore, the probabilities steadily increase towards 0.50 over the four resolution dates (1 week, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months).
